Triticum aestivum, commonly known as wheat, is a cereal grain that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae). The health benefits of wheat include providing essential nutrients, fiber, and energy to the human body, as well as potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of wheat include ferulic acid, a phenolic compound with antioxidant properties, as well as other phenolic acids, flavonoids, and lignans. Wheat is often used to make various herbal preparations, including wheat bran tea, wheat germ oil, and wheat flour-based supplements, which are commonly used to support digestive health and provide a source of essential nutrients.
